extra.reasoning: The property requires that rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never jointly true. The only way rec_drug_a becomes true is rule r4, whose guard requires elderly=true (plus renal_impairment, low_ejection_fraction, bradycardia, g6pd_deficiency all true). But rule r1 sets rec_drug_b:=true whenever elderly=true. Since r1 and r4 target different outputs (rec_drug_b vs rec_drug_a), priority/conflict resolution never suppresses one in favor of the other — both fire independently. Therefore every input that triggers r4 (making rec_drug_a true) simultaneously triggers r1 (making rec_drug_b true), so rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b are both true. Concrete counterexample: elderly=renal_impairment=low_ejection_fraction=bradycardia=g6pd_deficiency=true, hepatic_impairment=seizure_history=false. Then r4 fires (rec_drug_a=true) and r1 fires (rec_drug_b=true), violating mutual exclusion. The property is violated.
